title: Apply to 5 Internships This Week
body: Pick 5 companies in your field. Find their intern programs. Apply even if you feel underqualified. Getting 1 callback from 5 applications is normal — not failure.

title: Set Up Your LinkedIn Profile Today
body: A completed LinkedIn profile is your digital handshake. Add your school, major, any projects, and a professional photo. Then connect with 5 classmates and one professor.

title: Research Salary Before Your Next Interview
body: Use Levels.fyi, LinkedIn Salary, or Glassdoor to find the range for your role and city. Write down your floor — the minimum you'd accept — before you walk in.

title: Message One Alum This Week
body: Find a woman who graduated from your school and works in your field. Send a short message: who you are, what you're pursuing, and one specific question. Most people want to help.

title: Add One Project to Your Resume
body: Class project, personal project, or club work — it counts. Describe it in two bullet points: what you built and what the impact was. Quantify if possible.

title: Ask Your Professor About Research
body: Research opportunities are often unpaid but lead to publications, grad school letters, and network connections. Email one professor this week. Ask if they have student openings.

title: Prepare Your 60-Second Intro
body: Write and rehearse a 60-second version of: who you are, what you study, what you want to do, and why. Use it at every networking event and career fair this semester.

title: Join a Women's Professional Community
body: Find one women-focused professional club or online community in your field. AnitaB.org, Women in Finance, WiCyS — these communities share jobs, advice, and referrals.

title: Practice One Behavioral Interview Answer
body: Use the STAR format: Situation, Task, Action, Result. Pick a challenge you've overcome and practice telling it in 90 seconds. Record yourself. Watch it once.

title: Learn to Read a Job Description
body: Required = must have. Preferred = nice to have. Apply if you meet 60%+ of required skills. Many women wait until they meet 100%. The gap is the gap. Apply now.
